CVE-2025-32801.title: Kea configuration and API directives can be used to load a malici ...
CVE-2025-32801.description:
Kea configuration and API directives can be used to load a malicious hook library.  Many common configurations run Kea as root, leave the API entry points unsecured by default, and/or place the control sockets in insecure paths.
This issue affects Kea versions 2.4.0 through 2.4.1, 2.6.0 through 2.6.2, and 2.7.0 through 2.7.8.

CVE-2025-32802.title: Kea configuration and API directives can be used to overwrite arb ...
CVE-2025-32802.description:
Kea configuration and API directives can be used to overwrite arbitrary files, subject to permissions granted to Kea.  Many common configurations run Kea as root, leave the API entry points unsecured by default, and/or place the control sockets in insecure paths.
This issue affects Kea versions 2.4.0 through 2.4.1, 2.6.0 through 2.6.2, and 2.7.0 through 2.7.8.

CVE-2025-32803.title: In some cases, Kea log files or lease files may be world-readable ...
CVE-2025-32803.description:
In some cases, Kea log files or lease files may be world-readable.
This issue affects Kea versions 2.4.0 through 2.4.1, 2.6.0 through 2.6.2, and 2.7.0 through 2.7.8.

CVE-2026-3608.title: Sending a maliciously crafted message to the kea-ctrl-agent, kea- ...
CVE-2026-3608.description:
Sending a maliciously crafted message to the kea-ctrl-agent, kea-dhcp-ddns, kea-dhcp4, or kea-dhcp6 daemons over any configured API socket or HA listener can cause the receiving daemon to exit with a stack overflow error.
This issue affects Kea versions 2.6.0 through 2.6.4 and 3.0.0 through 3.0.2.
